label: Russian Military Casualties
contestNote: Russia has never released official casualty figures. Ukrainian MoD reported ~1,323,460 total personnel losses by Apr 24, 2026 (~910 casualties on April 23 alone). Mediazona/BBC confirmed 206,202 deaths by name as of Mar 30, 2026. Kremlin's classified assessments reportedly put KIA+WIA at 1,315,000 per Ukrainian HUR.
deltaNote: Western estimate since Nov 2025; ~910 losses Apr 23; cumulative total ~1,323,460

label: Ukrainian Military Casualties
contestNote: Zelenskyy stated 55,000 killed (2026); UALosses project documented 86,142 by name + 89,000+ MIA. CSIS estimates 100K–140K killed. Western officials (Feb 2026): 250K–300K total KIA+WIA.

label: Verified Civilian Deaths (UN)
contestNote: UN emphasizes real figures are considerably higher due to underreporting. 2025 was deadliest year for civilians since 2022; 31% rise over 2024.

label: Ukrainian Territory Under Russian Control
contestNote: Russia annexed Donetsk, Luhansk, Zaporizhzhia, and Kherson oblasts in Sep 2022, but doesn't fully control all four. Heavy fighting ongoing in Pokrovsk, Kostiantynivka, Lyman sectors. Russian advances in Zaporizhzhia oblast now ~7 km from provincial capital.
deltaNote: Slower than 2025 average of 171 sq mi/month; spring offensive pressure intensifying

label: Displaced Ukrainians
deltaNote: Plus 3.7M internally displaced; 10.8M in need of humanitarian assistance in 2026

label: Frozen Russian State Assets
contestNote: Primarily held in Euroclear, Belgium (~€210B). G7 using interest (~$3B/year) to back $50B Ukraine loan. EU maintains freeze; US Treasury issued 30-day oil waiver in Mar 2026.

label: Total Western Aid to Ukraine
contestNote: Includes US, EU, Germany, UK, Canada and other allies. US-Ukraine minerals deal signed Apr 2025 counts future US military aid as fund contributions. NATO targeting additional $15B in 2026.
deltaNote: Largest single US package; minerals deal signed Apr 30, 2025
